task_completion.description: Verify the initial tool lending data is loaded into LibreOffice Calc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc is open showing 8 rows of tool data with columns for Tool Name, Borrower, Date Lent, Expected Return, and Value.

task_completion.description: Verify a 'Days Out' column has been added with date calculations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column (e.g., 'Days Out') exists and contains integer values representing the number of days since the 'Date Lent'. The formula bar may show a formula like =TODAY()-C2.

task_completion.description: Verify a 'Status' column has been added using conditional formulas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column (e.g., 'Status') exists and contains text values 'OVERDUE' or 'OK'. The formula bar may show an IF formula like =IF(F2>30,"OVERDUE","OK").

task_completion.description: Verify the correct number of items are identified as OVERDUE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly 5 of the 8 tools should have the status 'OVERDUE' in the Status column, while the remaining 3 should be 'OK'.

task_completion.description: Verify red formatting is applied to OVERDUE items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cells containing the word 'OVERDUE' are formatted in red (either red text font or red cell background).

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved in ODS format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Calc window title bar shows the file name ending in '.ods' (e.g., tool_lending_result.ods), or a 'Save As' dialog is visible selecting the ODF Spreadsheet (.ods) format.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the LibreOffice Calc GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreOffice Calc application interface being actively used (menus, cell selection, formula bar), not just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used formulas rather than hardcoding values.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a 'Days Out' or 'Status' cell is selected, the formula bar at the top should display a formula (e.g., =TODAY()-... or =IF(...)) rather than plain text like '45' or 'OVERDUE'.

integrity.description: Verify formatting was applied using spreadsheet features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The red formatting should appear within the Calc grid. Evidence of using the Conditional Formatting dialog or standard formatting toolbar is a strong indicator of genuine interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial data contains exactly 8 tools. The agent must add a 'Days Out' column calculating days since 'Date Lent' (e.g., =TODAY()-C2) and a 'Status' column using an IF formula with a >30 days threshold (e.g., =IF(F2>30,"OVERDUE","OK")). By design, exactly 5 tools evaluate to OVERDUE. The OVERDUE cells must be formatted in red. The final file must be saved in ODS format.
